page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 52 GB of system memory, yielding 6.5 GB of RAM per core. The underlying hardware is powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This configuration consists of 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is dedicated, and the processor includes a 45 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the n1-highmem-8 delivers modest processing capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng place the instance in the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds fall into the bottom 10% of tested hardware. However, memory bandwidth performance is more resilient, with large-block read and write operations achieving average tier results. Passmark CPU tests for encryption and physics also register in the middle 50% tier, while database operations and Redis SET throughput remain in the bottom 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the n1-highmem-8 offers a qualitative advantage for workloads requiring higher memory-to-CPU ratios without the premium cost of newer generation memory-optimized instances. Because its compute performance is relatively weak, it is less suitable for CPU-intensive tasks like heavy compilation or complex cryptography. Instead, the instance is best suited for memory-bound workloads that do not demand high-frequency processing, such as caching layers, moderate database operations, and lightweight web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 52 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 6.5 GB. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate modest compute capabilities, with single-core and multi-core stress-ng scores in the bottom 25%, and single-threaded compression speeds in the bottom 10%. However, the instance maintains average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and Passmark CPU encryption tests. Given its high memory allocation relative to its processing power, the n1-highmem-8 is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads. It is best suited for caching systems, moderate database operations, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs, 52 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute requirements.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering a high memory-to-CPU ratio for memory-bound workloads requiring moderate processing power.
